title: EPA v. Calumet Shreveport Refining Case on SRE Challenges Heard at Supreme Court
content: During the Supreme Court hearing on EPA v. Calumet Shreveport Refining, L.L.C. on March 25, 2025, the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) defended its mass denial of Small Refinery Exemptions. Citing a new law interpretation and introducing the "passthrough theory," the EPA argued that refineries could pass compliance costs to customers. To avoid inconsistent rulings across circuits and conserve judicial resources, the EPA sought centralized review in the D.C. Circuit Court.
author: Analysts Account
source: Supreme Court of the United States
